response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned web and backend engineer with 15+ years of broad experience, but fails to meet several hard requirements for this role. Most critically, no Japanese language proficiency is listed — a stated non-negotiable requirement. Beyond that disqualifier, the candidate has no meaningful experience in embedded networking, LAN-switch development, security protocols (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange), or Linux kernel modification. C programming appears only briefly in their history (~6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015), while their primary and sustained expertise is TypeScript, JavaScript, and Node.js — a stack largely irrelevant to embedded firmware for network switches.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse software development experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Demonstrated software craftsmanship discipline including TDD, CI/CD, and low-latency systems work (D-language async I/O at Sociomantic)
Some exposure to low-level programming (C/C++ at National Instruments, robotic arm hardware at Altom, Linux usage throughout career)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — an explicit, non-negotiable hard requirement for this position
Zero experience with the required security domain: no MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, MAC authentication, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange, or device attack/tamper detection
C programming is minimal and dated (6 months in 2014–2015 under the skills list it is not even mentioned); no Linux kernel development or source-level modification experience evident

key_factors.factor: No security or embedded networking expertise
